text: Citywide traffic forecasting is essential for a smart transportation system. Most works focus on discovering the underlying spatial-temporal relationships, but there are few explorations on the underlying assumptions and limitations. Recently, many works assume that all regions have the same distribution and that the temporal heterogeneity caused by different traffic patterns at different time slots can be modeled via a uniform parameterized space, which may not be the case in real-world scenarios. To address the above challenges, we introduce a Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) method that aims to model traffic patterns that capture spatial and temporal heterogeneity with the aid of self-supervised learning (SSL) for traffic forecasting. First, we design an adaptive augmentation method for modeling the ST self-supervised learning through attribute-level and structure-level augmentations on the traffic flow graph in each attribute and structure, which enhances the representations of the underlying traffic patterns. Then, based on the augmented graphs, we build two auxiliary SSL tasks on top of the main task to enforce the spatial and temporal heterogeneity-aware augmentations, which further boost the representations of the underlying traffic patterns. Experimental evaluations on four large-scale datasets demonstrate the superiority of ST-SSL over state-of-the-art methods. Note that ST-SSL may benefit other spatial-temporal-related applications since the heterogeneity is widely observed. Our implemented code is available at https://github.com/Echo-Ji/ST-SSL.
